title: 🎙️ Working Lands & Industry
description: Today's focus is the resource economy that defines the Cariboo — forestry, ranching, mining, and the agricultural technology helping these industries adapt and modernize. We're looking for stories about the people and innovations keeping rural BC's working landscape viable, including tensions between resource extraction and long-term stewardship, and the technology bridging both. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today's focus is the resource economy that defines the Cariboo — forestry, ranching, mining, and the agricultural technology helping these industries adapt and modernize. We're looking for stories about the people and innovations keeping rural BC's working landscape viable, including tensions between resource extraction and long-term stewardship, and the technology bridging both.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Working Lands & Industry' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: precision forestry and remote sensing in Cariboo logging, AgTech and ranch management systems, mining automation and safety technology, farm sensors and soil monitoring, sawmill modernization and automation, drone surveys for resource management, IoT in agriculture and ranching, climate adaptation tech for working lands, workforce training for industrial tech, commodity tracking and supply chain tech, and how Cariboo resource industries adopt innovation. Prefer articles that bridge resource economy AND technology in rural/industrial contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [New Atlas] Startup harnesses the power of AI in the ongoing hunt for minerals
items.content_html:
It is said that during the gold rush, people were so focused on gold that they forgot the “goldmine” that was shovels. In the AI and clean energy boom, where infrastructure and applications are the focus, one company is betting on the most fundamental of shovels: raw materials. Introducing Earth AI, an Australian-founded, US-headquartered mineral exploration company that uses artificial intelligence to discover critical mineral deposits faster and more efficiently.

Continue Reading

Category: AI and Humanoids, Technology

Tags: Artificial Intelligence, Mining, Metals

items.title: [TechCrunch] Thinking Machines wants to build an AI that actually listens while it talks
items.content_html: Right now, every AI model you've ever used works the same way. You talk, it listens. It responds, you listen. Thinking Machines is trying to change that by building a model that processes your input and generates a response at the same time, so it's more like a phone call than a text chain.

items.title: [MacRumors] OpenAI's New Daybreak Platform Uses GPT-5.5 to Find Software Vulnerabilities
items.content_html:
OpenAI today launched Daybreak, an answer to Anthropic's Project Glasswing initiative and Mythos AI model. Like Glasswing, Daybreak is a cyber defense effort that will help tech companies find security vulnerabilities in their platforms.


OpenAI says Daybreak is aimed at building cyber defense into software from the start. It builds on OpenAI's April launch of GPT-5.4-Cyber, which the company says has contributed to fixing more than 3,000 vulnerabilities.
Daybreak combines the intelligence of OpenAI models, the extensibility of Codex as an agentic harness, and our partners across the security flywheel to help make the world safer for everyone. Defenders can bring secure code review, threat modeling, patch validation, dependency risk analysis, detection, and remediation guidance into the everyday development loop so software becomes more resilient from the start.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said OpenAI would like to work with "as many companies as possible" to help them continuously secure their software against cyber threats. Several companies have already adopted Anthropic's competing Glasswing program, including Apple, Microsoft, Google, and Amazon.

Daybreak uses Codex Security to build an editable threat model from a company's software repository, then it automates monitoring for higher-risk vulnerabilities. Issues that are found can be investigated in an isolated environment.

Companies can request a Daybreak assessment from OpenAI, which includes a vulnerability scan. Pricing is not listed.

There are three models available. GPT-5.5 has standard safeguards for general purpose use, while GPT-5.5 with Trusted Access for Cyber is meant for verified defensive work in authorized environments. GPT-5.5-Cyber is for specialized authorized workflows, and it features stronger verification and account-level controls.

OpenAI is working with industry and government partners ahead of deploying more cyber-capable models in the future.
Tag: OpenAI

This article, "OpenAI's New Daybreak Platform Uses GPT-5.5 to Find Software Vulnerabilities" first appeared on MacRumors.com
